---
title: "BUS 411"
subtitle: "Fixed Income Security Analysis and Valuation"
---

## Course Overview

Welcome to **BUS 411: Fixed Income Security Analysis and Valuation**.

As an introduction to the market valuation of fixed income securities, this course covers: history of fixed income securities; valuation methods for fixed income securities, including fixed and variable annuities, government and corporate bonds, defined benefit pension plans, bonds with contingencies, fixed income derivative securities; and, fixed income risk management using duration and convexity measures. 

Prerequisite: BUS 315 and BUS 360W, both with a minimum grade of C-; 60 units. 

## Course Materials

- **[Lecture Notes](lecture_notes/index.qmd)** — Weekly lecture notes with concepts, definitions, examples, and exercises.
- **[Assignments](assignments/index.qmd)** — Problem sets, labs, and other coursework.

## Getting Started

Browse the [Lecture Notes](lecture_notes/index.qmd) to access all course content. Each lecture is available in HTML on this site and can also be rendered to PDF for download.

::: {.callout-note}
## Materials Update
Course materials will be updated over the course of the semester. Please check back regularly for the latest content.
:::
